title: Labor market impacts of AI: A new measure and early evidence
snippet: Anthropic's Economic Research team introduces a new measure for tracking AI's impact on labor markets with early empirical evidence. This research provides quantitative analysis of how AI adoption affects employment patterns and workforce dynamics across different sectors.

title: From games to biology and beyond: 10 years of AlphaGo's impact
snippet: Ten years since AlphaGo defeated Lee Sedol, DeepMind explores how the breakthrough is catalyzing scientific discovery across biology, materials science, and paving a path toward AGI. The retrospective examines AlphaGo's influence on reinforcement learning research and its applications beyond game-playing to real-world scientific problems.

title: Launch HN: Kita (YC W26) – Automate credit review in emerging markets
snippet: Kita uses VLM-based agents to automate credit review for lenders in emerging markets like Philippines and Mexico. The system parses messy financial documents (bank statements, payslips) in any format, detects fraud, and extracts underwriting signals. Supports 50+ document types across PDFs, scans, photos. In 2025, $13.3T was lent globally with 90% involving document review. Kita's architecture is model-agnostic and trains on hyperlocalized credit signals in each market.

title: Demystifying Video Reasoning: Chain-of-Steps mechanism in diffusion models
snippet: Research reveals that reasoning in video generation models emerges along diffusion denoising steps rather than sequentially across frames (Chain-of-Steps vs Chain-of-Frames). Models explore multiple candidate solutions in early denoising steps and progressively converge to final answer. Identifies emergent behaviors: working memory, self-correction, perception before action. Discovers functional specialization within Diffusion Transformers where early layers encode perceptual structure, middle layers execute reasoning, later layers consolidate representations.

title: Efficient Reasoning on the Edge: LoRA adapters for mobile LLM reasoning
snippet: Qualcomm AI Research proposes lightweight approach to enable reasoning in small LLMs using LoRA adapters with supervised fine-tuning. Introduces budget forcing via reinforcement learning to reduce response length with minimal accuracy loss. Exploits parallel test-time scaling for memory-bound decoding. Presents dynamic adapter-switching mechanism that activates reasoning only when needed and KV-cache sharing strategy during prompt encoding. Experiments on Qwen2.5-7B demonstrate efficient, accurate reasoning under strict resource constraints for mobile devices.
